steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question. The task is to find the choice that best captures the main idea of the whole passage — not a supporting detail, not a misrepresentation, but the central claim the author is making.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Smart-Grosvenor's unconventional book Vibration Cooking: or, the Travel Notes of a Geechee Girl is an important contribution to food writing.: The passage calls the book her “most influential project,” calls it “unusual for its time,” and says it “shaped contemporary approaches to writing about cuisine.” Both “unconventional” and “important contribution to food writing” are directly supported. ✓
  • Choice B — Smart-Grosvenor held many different positions over her life, including reporter and food writer.: The passage mentions her TV and radio work only to downplay it (“may be known for”) before pivoting to the book. Listing her jobs is not the main idea. ✗
  • Choice C — Smart-Grosvenor's groundbreaking book Vibration Cooking: or, the Travel Notes of a Geechee Girl didn't receive the praise it deserved when it was first published in 1970.: The passage explicitly states it is “long admired by many.” The claim of under-appreciation is directly contradicted. ✗
  • Choice D — Smart-Grosvenor was a talented chef whose work inspired many people to start cooking for themselves.: The passage calls her a culinary anthropologist, not a chef, and says her book influenced food writing discourse — not home cooking. ✗
That is the correct answer.
